rendered.title: Browser Use takes over changelog media, plus DeepSeek and GPT‑5.5 model support
rendered.intro: Today's nightly is dominated by a major rewrite of how Aura produces release changelog media: the old Browserbase/Playwright screenshot pipeline was retired in favor of a Browser Use–driven flow gated by Anthropic planning and OpenAI vision checks. Alongside that infrastructure overhaul, the model catalog picked up DeepSeek direct pricing and a new GPT‑5.5 entry, and the Aura desktop apps gained richer agent-facing semantics for capture.

entries.title: Changelog media pipeline rebuilt around Browser Use
entries.summary: The legacy Browserbase/Playwright screenshot system was removed and replaced with a leaner Browser Use planning flow, fronted by new quality and branding gates and a stateless capture auth path on the server.

items.text: Retired the old changelog media stack — including the publish, retry, and history-sync workflows, the demo screenshot runner, and the Browserbase-based capture scripts — and introduced a Browser Use trial runner, a media planner, and a navigation-contract sitemap as the new foundation (net ~16k lines removed).

Added structural quality and branding gates so a screenshot must pass proof checks before a branded SVG card is generated, and the branded asset itself must clear separate layout checks before it's considered publish-ready.

Hardened the planner so large changelogs are chunked instead of truncated, omitted entries get a rescue pass, and Browser Use is only invoked after Anthropic classifies an entry as desktop, visual, sitemap-backed, and worth capturing.

Reworked capture auth on the server with a new stateless `capture_auth` module and seeded capture sessions, and added a dedicated CaptureLoginView so Browser Use runs can authenticate without a persistent profile.

entries.title: Safer handoff from media evaluation to publish
entries.summary: A small follow-up formalized how an evaluated media candidate is passed to the publishing step.

items.text: Added an explicit publish handoff stage in the media evaluation pipeline and documented it in the Browser Use plan, so only candidates that cleared all gates are forwarded for publication.

entries.title: Publish workflow, vision gates, and Fireworks pricing
entries.summary: The afternoon batch reintroduced an end-to-end publish workflow on top of the new pipeline, switched the final vision gate to OpenAI, and expanded benchmark pricing coverage for Fireworks open models.

items.text: Rebuilt a Publish Release Changelog Media GitHub workflow that triggers off the upstream changelog publish, plus a `publish-changelog-media.mjs` script and high-resolution capture helper to drive end-to-end media generation.
Moved the final media vision gate to OpenAI so the branded PNG is judged by the same model used for branding, and added a hard reject for empty-state or low-content proofs.

Tightened Browser Use behavior with a 2560×1440 desktop viewport floor, sensitive-secret handling for the capture key form, and explicit session creation with keep-alive so captures don't fall back to a weak proof.
Simplified the media card branding layout and added Fireworks open-model pricing coverage to benchmark tooling and the model catalog.

entries.title: DeepSeek direct provider and seeded capture plans
entries.summary: The model catalog gained a first-class DeepSeek path, the deprecated Fireworks DeepSeek V3.2 entry was dropped, and capture runs got scalable per-candidate seed plans wired into the Aura 3D apps.

items.text: Added DeepSeek as a direct provider — including DEEPSEEK_API_KEY plumbing, a deepseek-v4-flash default, harness routing for `aura-deepseek-v4*` models, and benchmark pricing/usage support for DeepSeek's prompt cache hit/miss token fields.
Removed the Fireworks-hosted DeepSeek V3.2 model from the catalog, runtime, and pricing tables now that DeepSeek is wired up directly.

Introduced per-candidate capture seed plans and a `capture-bridge` in the interface, with new App Shell wiring and updates across the Aura 3D main, sidekick, image generation, and image preview panels so seeded sessions can deep-link into the right surface.

media.alt: DeepSeek direct provider and seeded capture plans
media.caption: DeepSeek is now a direct provider, with deepseek-v4-flash available in the chat model picker.

entries.title: GPT‑5.5 support and tighter agent-facing semantics
entries.summary: Late-night work added GPT‑5.5 to the model lineup, taught the navigation contract about new agent context handles, and calibrated the high-resolution capture path with a curated lessons knowledge base.

items.text: Added GPT‑5.5 as a selectable model with benchmark pricing entries and chat input wiring.

Extended the Aura navigation contract to recognize `data-agent-context`, `data-agent-context-anchor`, and `data-agent-proof` handles so capture targets bound the visible product boundary instead of grabbing oversized containers, with matching attributes added across Agent, Aura 3D, model generation, and chat input components.
Calibrated the compact high-resolution capture path and seeded a curated `lessons.json` knowledge base that the planner consults to avoid repeating known failure modes.

media.alt: GPT‑5.5 support and tighter agent-facing semantics
media.caption: GPT-5.5 is now available as a selectable model in the Agents chat composer.

highlights: Old screenshot pipeline replaced by Browser Use planning
Layered quality, branding, and vision gates for media
DeepSeek direct pricing and GPT‑5.5 added to model catalog
2560×1440 desktop captures with seeded auth sessions
